authoritative_sources.title: Process Safety
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's process-safety overview is authoritative public guidance for what the PSM rule covers, how covered facilities should think about catastrophic-release prevention, and which records govern operator-side process-safety support.

authoritative_sources.title: Process Safety Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA 3132 is authoritative public operating guidance for process safety information, mechanical-integrity inspection and testing records, hot work permits, incident investigations, and three-year compliance-audit expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control, maintenance, and permit evidence review
used_for: inspection, test, and deficiency record requirements

authoritative_sources.title: CPL 02-01-065 Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This OSHA enforcement manual is authoritative for how process-safety evidence, hot-work permit auditing, technical interpretations, and documentation sufficiency are reviewed during PSM enforcement.

authoritative_sources.title: PSM Covered Chemical Facilities National Emphasis Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's National Emphasis Program is authoritative for current inspection targeting and confirms that threshold-quantity chemical facilities are reviewed against PSM obligations using structured enforcement workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Program (RMP) Rule Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's RMP overview is authoritative public guidance for threshold-triggered accident-prevention, emergency-response, and risk-management-plan expectations at covered facilities.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR Part 68 - Chemical Accident Prevention Provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for threshold quantities, Program 2 and Program 3 prevention-program elements, hot work permits, management of change, pre-startup review, compliance audits, incident investigation, emergency response, and recordkeeping.
used_for: five-year accident history and recordkeeping expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Plan (RMP) Amendments Compliance Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's compliance-information page is authoritative for implementing current RMP expectations around compliance audits, incident investigation, emergency coordination, and exercises that shape downstream evidence handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Program (RMP) Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's RMP program page is authoritative for current program status, submission cadence, public guidance access, and the active 2026 rulemaking posture that could change future implementation details.
used_for: nonblocking ambiguity tracking for active rulemaking

workflow_stages.name: covered-process intake and boundary triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify facility, process, threshold posture, covered chemical, startup or maintenance context, and whether the request stays inside process-safety evidence support before making recommendations.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: control, threshold, and prerequisite mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map PSM and RMP obligations, safeguards, permits, audits, and owner responsibilities to the exact process, asset, and incident context so hold or release logic stays evidence-backed.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: evidence assembly and inspection review
workflow_stages.description: Collect and evaluate PSI, PHA, MOC, PSSR, work permits, MI records, contractor files, incident logs, and audit findings needed to support a disposition.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and restart boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible evidence supports maintaining hold, recommending bounded release readiness, escalating investigation, or blocking startup or return to service without implying final authority.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: incident, changed-condition, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate repeat incidents, threshold uncertainty, safeguard impairment, field conditions, emergency-response implications, or requests for engineering and regulator-side signoff that move authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

artifacts: process safety information, P&IDs, relief-system basis records, and safe operating limit documents
PHA, HAZOP, LOPA, and action-item trackers
management-of-change packets and pre-startup safety review checklists
mechanical-integrity inspection, test, calibration, and deficiency records
hot work, line break, confined space, and energy-isolation permits
contractor qualification records and turnaround work packs
incident investigations, near-miss logs, compliance-audit reports, and five-year accident history records
emergency-coordination records, response drills, and regulator or insurer inspection findings

allowed: classify process-safety requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
prepare evidence-backed safety status summaries, hold or nonconformance records, incident summaries, and escalation packets tied to named process, permit, and asset records
map threshold, permit, inspection, startup, contractor, and audit dependencies to the correct owner without improvising engineering, emergency-command, or regulator authority
escalate: required tenant facts, threshold data, process records, field conditions, or source context are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, jurisdictional, and tenant-policy conflicts materially change whether a process may start, continue, return to service, or close an incident

repeat incidents, safeguard impairments, changed field conditions, overdue audits, or emergency-response implications require engineer-of-record, plant leadership, EHS, legal, or regulator-reporting review
refuse: requests for engineering design approval, relief or SIS setting approval, legal interpretation, emergency-command decisions, regulator negotiation, or final signoff
requests to start up, restart, continue operating, or close an incident when required safeguards, permits, audits, investigations, or approval gates are incomplete or disputed
requests that would bypass documented hot-work, line-break, isolation, MOC, PSSR, MI, or incident-investigation prerequisites

domain_failure_modes: Treating incomplete threshold-quantity or interconnected-process data as sufficient to declare a unit outside PSM or RMP scope.
Allowing startup or continued operation even though MOC actions, PSSR items, proof tests, calibration records, or critical permits are missing, stale, or contradictory.
Collapsing changed field conditions such as corrosion, alarm impairment, bypasses, or new leaks into minor notes instead of answer-changing process-safety events.
Conflating process-safety evidence review with engineer-of-record judgment, safe operating limit approval, relief-system design authority, or regulator-facing certification.
Treating a three-year compliance audit or incident investigation as satisfied when closure evidence is missing or repeat-event patterns remain unresolved.
Using tenant policy to override stricter manufacturer, RAGAGEP, or regulator-backed inspection expectations without escalation.
Producing generic safety advice that ignores covered-process scope, startup gating, catastrophic-release prevention, and process-specific records of authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Facility-specific threshold determination and process interconnection logic
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public OSHA and EPA sources define threshold-based applicability, but each tenant must supply the current covered-chemical inventory, concentration assumptions, and interconnected-process boundaries before a case-specific applicability conclusion is safe.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: EPA RMP rulemaking status after the February 24, 2026 proposal
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: EPA's program page shows active 2026 proposed revisions. Until a final rule changes the controlling text, runtime should treat the current 40 CFR Part 68 baseline and tenant legal guidance as controlling for execution.
